description_long: This is a roughly rectangular, irregularly shaped stone with a pale, off-white coloration and a coarse, weathered surface. The stone features a slightly concave top with a shallow groove and several small pitted marks, suggesting natural erosion or wear. One side is relatively flat and bears a visible handwritten catalog number, indicating it may have been part of a collection or survey. The other sides are more rugged, with undulating surfaces and subtle textural variations, including some minor chips and indentations. The stone's overall form is blocky but asymmetrical, with one edge tapering and curving inward. Its size and mass suggest it could serve as a physical barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, such as a park or sidewalk.
description: A pale, irregularly shaped stone with a rough, weathered surface and a visible catalog number on one side. Its blocky, asymmetrical form and coarse texture make it suitable as an urban obstacle or barrier.
description_view_0: The front view shows a broad, slightly concave face with a shallow groove and small pitted marks, highlighting the stone's weathered texture.
description_view_1: This side view reveals the stone's pronounced curvature and tapering edge, emphasizing its irregular, block-like shape.
description_view_2: The back view displays a relatively flat surface with a handwritten catalog number, contrasting with the more rugged sides.
description_view_3: This opposite side view shows the stone's undulating, rough surface and the inward curve along one edge.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular pitting and shallow grooves with no artificial carvings; natural, random surface texture.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered with minor chips and surface pitting, indicating prolonged exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or manicured urban surfaces but could blend with other natural stones or landscaping features.
